item.title: Blog
item.description: Sharpen your defenses with the latest threat insights, industry trends and product updates sure to put you one step ahead of hackers – and your competition.

item.title: Zero-Day Fireworks: Microsoft’s July Patch Party
item.description: Microsoft released its July 2023 Patch Tuesday updates, featuring 132 vulnerabilities. Six of them are zero days. Learn more from the APG.

item.title: Blackpoint Cyber Helps MSPs and SMB Customers Maximize Cybersecurity
item.description: Blackpoint helps MSPs using Microsoft Defender maximize their security investments with our SOC and MDR for the endpoint and the cloud.

item.title: SocGholish: Haunting the Digital Realm for Over Five Years
item.description: The SocGholish malware family has been around for years, using a variety of advanced tactics to elude detection. Learn more from the APG.

item.title: Exploding Back into the Picture: TeamTNT Makes a Comeback
item.description: TeamTNT, a hacking group, seems to have made a comeback with a fresh botnet campaign aimed at infiltrating cloud-native environments.

item.title: WormGPT: The New Bait in Phishing and BEC Attacks
item.description: Cybercriminals are maliciously using AI to auto-generate highly persuasive and personalized fake emails to avoid being flagged as suspicious.

item.title: FIN8's Latest Remix: Sardonic with Noberus
item.description: FIN8 has new TTPs, utilizing a modified version of the Sardonic backdoor to deliver the Noberus ransomware. Learn more from the APG.

item.title: Identity Crisis in the Cloud: Storm-0558 and the Forged Access Key
item.description: Examine research regarding a significant security incident, attributed to China, affecting multiple Exchange Online & Outlook.com customers.

item.title: In-House Technology Ensures Speed, Security, and Synchronization
item.description: Add detection and response capabilities to your cybersecurity arsenal within minutes. Learn more about SNAP-Defense, the in-house technology that powers Blackpoint’s cybersecurity ecosystem.

item.title: How Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) Attacks Go Down
item.description: RDP attacks aren't new, but are becoming increasingly common. Used mainly to target smaller businesses, RDP can be a serious liability if improperly configured. Learn how to avoid insecurities here.

item.title: Examining the Conti Group, Leaks & Evolving Ransomware
item.description: After a leak of 60K documents, the infosec community got a glimpse into the Conti Group. In this blog, Blackpoint examines the leak’s role in the evolution of ransomware in the threat landscape.

item.title: The State of Cloud Security: Zero Trust and Managed Response
item.description: The state of cloud security is shifting as the scope of both its usage and its power continues to expand. Businesses should create security strategies around cloud implementation to ensure sustainability and efficiency. Maintain your cloud security with Zero Trust Architecture and Blackpoint's purpose-build cloud security solution. Read on to learn more.

item.title: 5 Reasons You Won’t Want to Miss ReCON 2022
item.description: As we count down the days till ReCON, we're also counting the reasons why you should attend. Blackpoint is providing the cyber community with a free day of learning from the experts. Empower your company with cyber threat intel, valuable networking, MSP sales training, and innovative technology! Read more about it and register here.

item.title: Don't Take the Bait This Summer: Remote Work, Social Media & Limited Staff
item.description: Security practices can become lax in the summer months, but that is actually when employees should be on high alert. Phishing attacks continue to be prevalent, using social media, remote work vulnerabilities, and decreased staff to penetrate business networks. Protect your company by implementing these practices and deploying Cloud Response to your network.

item.title: Blackpoint Road Trip 2022
item.description: The 2022 event season is upon us. Read our latest blog to see where you can talk shop with Blackpoint Cyber and the greater MSP community. Our team is finishing out the year with over 10 in-person events in the next six months. We look forward to reconnecting or meeting you there!

item.title: Midnight Blizzard Mixes Up Microsoft 365
item.description: A threat actor, Midnight Blizzard, conducts targeted attacks using credential theft phishing lures sent through Microsoft Teams.

item.title: Meet Rhysida: The 'Helpful' Cybersecurity Team with a Dark Side
item.description: Rhysida Ransomware Group first appeared in May & quickly established itself as a significant threat operation, targeting victims worldwide.

item.title: Viva Las Vulnerabilities: MGM Hit by ALPHV Ransomware
item.description: The ALPHV ransomware group successfully compromised MGM Resorts, using the unique tactics of LinkedIn and a phone call.

item.title: In the Chaos of Lorenz, APG sORted It Out
item.description: Blackpoint Cyber analyzed an attack conducted by the Lorenz ransomware group. Lorenz has been active since early 2021 and is known to leverage a vulnerability in MiVoice Connect. Read the blog for analysis of the malicious executable and the use of .NET DLLs.

item.title: Secure Your Prizes and Clients With Blackpoint Cyber
item.description: This holiday season, Blackpoint is offering two giveaways. The Holidaze is an opportunity for MSPs to win ten days of gifts, and SOC’ing Stuffers is a giveaway for those that book demos between Dec. 1-20. We hope these opportunities are a fun way to celebrate the holidays and get to know the Blackpoint ecosystem!

item.title: A Toolset to Protect You Every Step of the Way
item.description: Team up with Blackpoint and experience a streamlined, cohesive ecosystem that supports them every step of the cybersecurity journey.

item.title: With .one Foot in the Door
item.description: Blackpoint is continuing to respond to the OneNote malware found within phishing emails. The APG has released a blog featuring OneNote’s tactics, IoCs, and more.

item.title: 2023 Cyberthreats to Watch Out For
item.description: Our SOC & APG predict that many of last year's threats will persist in 2023. Read our blog for the top four cybersecurity threats this year.

item.title: A Deep Dive Into the 3CXDesktopApp Security Vulnerability
item.description: For a technical deep dive into the threat actor’s sophisticated techniques behind 3CX's security vulnerability, read the APG’s latest blog.

item.title: The EDR Evolution
item.description: Threat actors can surpass your AV/EDR with advanced tradecraft. Provide endpoint backup with Managed EDR. Read about it now.

item.title: Introducing Managed Application Control
item.description: Introducing our modern take on Zero Trust: Managed Application Control! Partner with Blackpoint’s expertise to streamline your security stack today.

item.title: An Update on the MOVEit Vulnerability
item.description: APG expands upon the SOC’s threat notice regarding the MOVEit Transfer critical zero-day vulnerability with research including web shell analysis.

item.title: The Competitive Edge of Managed Application Control
item.description: Discover the selling power of Managed Application Control for MSPs. Unify security efforts, optimize resources, and build a scalable security stack with Blackpoint today.

item.title: Sign Here, Malware There: Forged Kernel-Mode Driver Signatures
item.description: Read the latest APG Threat Intel on a security vulnerability in Windows that allows threat actors to sign and load malicious kernel-mode drivers.

item.title: Okta’s Social Engineering Escalations
item.description: APG analysis of an Okta article discussing social engineering attacks targeting highly privileged roles within their customer organizations.
